  "findings": [
    "Worn sheave grooves causing the belt to ride too low in the groove (bottoming out)."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Replace the worn sheave before final commissioning.",
    "Inspect belt for premature wear and replace if damaged.",
    "Re-align and tension the new sheave-and-belt assembly."
  ],
  "rationale": "According to manufacturer guides, belts must ride at the proper height in the sheave grooves to transmit power efficiently via wedging action. A belt riding low indicates severe groove wear, which leads to slippage, rapid belt wear, and loss of drive efficiency.",

    "mfr guides"
  ]
